highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 delivers targeted performance wins and upstream Skia m151 bug fixes.
highlights_body: Hot paths in tag parsing and glyph shaping have been optimized with zero-copy spans and allocation-free overloads, and two rounds of upstream Skia m151 engine fixes have been merged.

bullets.lead: Skia m151 upstream bug fixes merged
bullets.detail: Two rounds of upstream Chrome m151 engine fixes have been pulled in, keeping the bundled renderer current with the latest Skia servicing patches.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing optimized with ReadOnlySpan overloads
bullets.detail: SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char> directly, eliminating string allocations in hot color-parsing paths.
The unnecessary unsafe modifier on GRContextOptions has been removed, making the type usable in projects that do not allow unsafe code.

bullets.lead: SKShaper glyph results read via zero-copy spans
bullets.detail: Reading shaping glyph results now uses Span-based access instead of element-by-element P/Invoke calls, eliminating per-glyph interop overhead.

bullets.lead: Per-call allocations removed from tag parsing
bullets.detail: SKFourByteTag.Parse and HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse no longer allocate a char[4] on every call, reducing GC pressure in layout-heavy workloads.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Added a cross-backend golden-image visual regression test harness to the test suite
